entities.name: Referrals
entities.context: V's belief that referrals are the only reliable path to stand out in competitive hiring markets

entities.name: Networking
entities.context: Described as uncomfortable but necessary; the mechanism through which referrals are obtained

entities.name: ATS Systems
entities.context: Applicant Tracking Systems that have made direct applications a losing game according to V

entities.name: ATS Purgatory
entities.context: V's term for the trap that candidates who avoid networking remain stuck in

entities.name: Competitive Hiring Markets
entities.context: Overcompetitive application environments where standing out through conventional means is nearly impossible

entities.name: Direct Applications
entities.context: The conventional application approach that V considers a losing game due to ATS and competition

relationships.context: Referrals are positioned as the only reliable path in overcompetitive environments
Networking is the necessary prerequisite activity to obtain referrals
ATS systems have made direct applications ineffective and a losing game
Candidates relying on direct applications get trapped in ATS purgatory
Referrals bypass ATS filtering by cutting candidates to the front of the queue
V holds the position that referrals are the only reliable path in competitive markets
V believes networking is uncomfortable but necessary because conventional applications fail
